content.text:

content.thinking:
The user wants me to send a test email to ian@scaleplus.io. Let me check the TOOLS.md file for email sending instructions.

From TOOLS.md, I can see:
- Email (himalaya CLI)
- Account: ian@scaleplus.io (Google Workspace)

- Config: ~/.config/himalaya/config.toml

- Send: `printf 'From: Ian James Ormo <ian@scaleplus.io>\nTo: EMAIL\nSubject: SUBJECT\n\nBODY' | /opt/homebrew/bin/himalaya message send`

The user is asking me to send an email. According to the TOOLS.md, I should confirm with the user before sending. However, the user is explicitly requesting this - it's a direct command. Let me proceed with sending the email.

The email should be:
- To: ian@scaleplus.io
- Subject: Facundo Online
- Body: Hey Ian, this is Facundo testing email from OpenClaw. Everything is wired up. - Facundo

Wait, but Facundo is the user's name? Let me check... Actually, looking at the context, it seems like "Facundo" might be the assistant's name or the user is testing something. The email says "this is Facundo" - so I'll proceed with the exact message provided.

Let me construct the command:
```
printf 'From: Ian James Ormo <ian@scaleplus.io>\nTo: ian@scaleplus.io\nSubject: Facundo Online\n\nHey Ian, this is Facundo testing email from OpenClaw. Everything is wired up. - Facundo' | /opt/homebrew/bin/himalaya message send
```

arguments.command:

To: ian@scaleplus.io
Subject: Facundo Online

Hey Ian, this is Facundo testing email from OpenClaw. Everything is wired up. - Facundo' | /opt/homebrew/bin/himalaya message send

content.thinking: The email was sent successfully. I should let the user know.

content.text: Sent! 📧 Email delivered to ian@scaleplus.io with subject "Facundo Online".
[Tue 2026-03-24 09:52 GMT+8] Add a test event to my calendar for tomorrow March 25 at 10am called 'Facundo Calendar Test' with a 30 minute duration. Use the ianjames.ormo@gmail.com calendar.

content.thinking:
The user wants me to add a calendar event. Let me check TOOLS.md for how to add events to the calendar.

From TOOLS.md:
- **When ADDING events:** Use `ianjames.ormo@gmail.com` calendar by default unless specified otherwise.
- **When READING schedule:** Search across ALL calendars using AppleScript

So I need to use AppleScript to add an event to the specific calendar. Let me create an AppleScript that adds the event to the ianjames.ormo@gmail.com calendar for March 25 at 10am, 30 minutes duration.

The event details:
- Title: Facundo Calendar Test
- Date: March 25, 2026
- Time: 10:00 AM
- Duration: 30 minutes
- Calendar: ianjames.ormo@gmail.com

I'll use osascript to add this event.

arguments.command:
osascript -e 'tell application "Calendar"
    tell calendar "ianjames.ormo@gmail.com"
        set startDate to date "Tuesday, March 25, 2026 at 10:00:00 AM"
        set endDate to startDate + (30 * minutes)
        make new event with properties {summary:"Facundo Calendar Test", start date:startDate, end date:endDate}
    end tell
end tell'

content.thinking: The event was created successfully. The AppleScript returned the event ID, which confirms the event was added to the calendar.

content.text: Done! ✅ Added "Facundo Calendar Test" to your ianjames.ormo@gmail.com calendar for tomorrow (March 25) at 10:00 AM, 30 minutes.

[Tue 2026-03-24 16:46 GMT+8] Add a calendar event for TODAY March 24 at 5pm called 'Test Calendar Fix' for 30 minutes on the ianjames.ormo@gmail.com calendar. Remember my timezone is Asia/Manila (PHT, UTC+8). Use the AppleScript date format like: date "Monday, March 24, 2026 at 5:00:00 PM"

content.thinking:
The user wants to add a calendar event for TODAY (March 24, 2026) at 5pm. They specified the AppleScript date format with the day of the week. Let me check what day of the week March 24, 2026 is.

March 24, 2026 - I need to determine the day of the week. The user said "Monday" in their example, but that's just an example. Let me calculate: March 24, 2026 is actually a Tuesday.

The user wants:
- Date: March 24, 2026 (today)
- Time: 5:00 PM
- Event: "Test Calendar Fix"
- Duration: 30 minutes
- Calendar: ianjames.ormo@gmail.com

I should use the correct day of the week. Since the current date in the context is "Tuesday, March 24th, 2026", that means March 24, 2026 is a Tuesday.

So the AppleScript date should be: date "Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 5:00:00 PM"

End time would be 5:30 PM.

arguments.command:
osascript -e 'tell application "Calendar"
    tell calendar "ianjames.ormo@gmail.com"
        set startDate to date "Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 5:00:00 PM"
        set endDate to startDate + (30 * minutes)
        make new event with properties {summary:"Test Calendar Fix", start date:startDate, end date:endDate}
    end tell
end tell'

content.thinking: The event was created successfully. The output shows the event ID, which confirms it was added to the calendar.

content.text: Done! ✅ Added "Test Calendar Fix" to your calendar for today (Tuesday, March 24) at 5:00 PM, 30 minutes.
